phases.goal: Define your target role and identify your competitive advantage

steps.title: Define your target role with precision
steps.description: Identify 3–5 specific job titles you're aiming for. Research what skills, experience, and credentials are required. Most career transitions fail because the target is too vague. 'Senior Product Manager at a B2B SaaS startup' beats 'something in product'.
steps.success_metric: 3–5 specific target roles defined with required skills mapped

steps.title: Audit your current skills and gaps
steps.description: Compare your current skillset against the requirements of your target role. Identify your top 3 transferable strengths and your top 3 gaps. Be honest — this drives everything that follows.
steps.success_metric: Skills audit completed with gaps and strengths documented

steps.title: Optimise your LinkedIn profile
steps.description: Recruiters search LinkedIn like a search engine. Optimise your headline (target role + value proposition), summary (story + achievements), and experience (results, not responsibilities). 70% of jobs are filled through connections.
steps.success_metric: LinkedIn headline, summary, and top 3 experiences rewritten

phases.goal: Close your key skill gaps and build external visibility

steps.title: Close your top skill gap
steps.description: Enrol in one targeted course, certification, or project to address your most critical skill gap. Choose something you can complete within 8 weeks and that produces a tangible output (certificate, portfolio piece, case study).
steps.success_metric: Course or certification enrolled and at least 75% completed

steps.title: Build a network in your target field
steps.description: Send 2 personalised connection requests per week to people in your target role or company. Ask for 15-minute informational interviews — not job leads. The goal is genuine relationships and insider knowledge, not transactions.
steps.success_metric: 16+ new connections made and at least 4 informational interviews completed

steps.title: Create one piece of visible work
steps.description: Write a LinkedIn article, build a side project, contribute to an open-source project, or give a talk at a meetup. Visible work builds credibility before any interview happens and makes your network reach out to you.
steps.success_metric: One piece of publicly visible work published

steps.title: Apply to 10 targeted roles
steps.description: Quality over quantity. Tailor each application to the specific role — reference the company's mission, use keywords from the job description, and quantify your impact with numbers wherever possible.

steps.title: Prepare and practise for interviews
steps.description: Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) for all behavioural questions. Prepare 5–7 strong stories. Do at least 3 mock interviews with a friend or recorded for self-review.
steps.success_metric: 5 STAR stories prepared and 3 mock interviews completed

steps.title: Negotiate your offer
steps.description: Never accept the first offer. Research market rates. Counter with a specific number backed by market data and your unique value. Negotiating your first offer has a compounding effect on every raise that follows.
steps.success_metric: Counter-offer made (outcome secondary to the attempt)
